mnemonics.title: P-H-P Basic Formula
mnemonics.content: If MORE pigeons than holes, then MULTIPLE in one hole. Formula: n > m → at least one hole has ceiling(n/m) pigeons.

mnemonics.title: Birthday Problem
mnemonics.content: 366 days = 366 holes. 367 people = 367 pigeons. Therefore at least 2 share a birthday (366+1 = 2 in one hole).

acronyms.expansion: Pigeonhole Principle - if n items go into m containers and n > m, then one container has more than one item
acronyms.usage: Used to prove existence without construction

acronyms.expansion: Ramsey Number - smallest number of people needed to guarantee m mutual acquaintances OR n mutual strangers
Ceiling function - rounds UP to nearest integer
acronyms.usage: Generalized pigeonhole: minimum in most filled box = ⌈n/m⌉

visualTips.visualization: Draw 5 circles (holes) and 7 dots (pigeons). Show distribution forcing at least one circle to have 2 dots.
Divide n items into m groups using equal distribution, remainder goes to first few groups - this shows ceiling value.
Stack 366 cards (days). Deal 367 cards (people) face down. Must have stack with 2+ cards - match found.
Complete graph K6 with all edges colored red/blue. Show any coloring guarantees monochrome triangle.

analogies.analogy: Putting 10 letters into 9 mailboxes - one mailbox gets 2 letters for sure.
Distributing 25 students into 4 hostels - one hostel gets at least 7 students (ceiling of 6.25).
Having 367 socks in 366 drawers of a drawer cabinet - definitely two socks in same drawer.
Drawing cards to get suit guarantee - worst case you get one short of each suit (12), 13th must match.
